sql-history-purpose.label: History and Purpose of SQL
sql-history-purpose.summary: Learn the origins and main uses of SQL in data management.

sketch-history-sql.title: The Origins of SQL
sketch-history-sql.bodyMarkdown: SQL, or Structured Query Language, was developed in the 1970s at IBM by Donald D. Chamberlin and Raymond F. Boyce. It was created to manage and retrieve data stored in IBM's first relational database system. The language became an ANSI standard in 1986 and an ISO standard in 1987, which helped it become the dominant language for relational databases.

sketch-purpose-sql.title: The Purpose of SQL
sketch-purpose-sql.bodyMarkdown: SQL is designed for managing and manipulating relational databases. Its main purposes include querying data, updating records, inserting new data, and deleting existing data. SQL allows users to interact with large datasets efficiently and is widely used in business, research, and technology for data analysis and reporting.

quiz-sql-origin.title: Who developed SQL?
quiz-sql-origin.prompt: Who were the main developers of SQL at IBM in the 1970s?

help.concept: SQL was created by researchers at IBM to manage relational databases.
help.hint_1: The developers were not from Microsoft or Google.
help.hint_2: Their names are Donald D. Chamberlin and Raymond F. Boyce.

options.a: Donald D. Chamberlin and Raymond F. Boyce

quiz-sql-purpose.title: Main Purposes of SQL
quiz-sql-purpose.prompt: Which of the following are main purposes of SQL? Select all that apply.

help.concept: SQL is used for interacting with relational databases, not for web design.
help.hint_1: SQL is about managing and manipulating data.
help.hint_2: Querying, updating, inserting, and deleting data are all core SQL tasks.

quiz-sql-standards.title: SQL Standardization
quiz-sql-standards.prompt: In which years did SQL become an ANSI and ISO standard?

help.concept: SQL was standardized to promote compatibility across database systems.
help.hint_2: ANSI standard came in 1986, ISO in 1987.

quiz-sql-uses.title: Fill in the Blank: SQL's Main Use
quiz-sql-uses.prompt: SQL is primarily used to manage and ______ data in relational databases.
quiz-sql-uses.hint: Think about accessing information from databases.

help.concept: SQL's main job is to get data out of databases.
help.hint_1: It's about getting information, not drawing or compiling.
help.hint_2: The word starts with 'r' and means to get data.

quiz-sql-uses.template: SQL is primarily used to manage and [[blank]] data in relational databases.

quiz-sql-query-example.title: Simple SQL Query Example
quiz-sql-query-example.prompt: Write an SQL query to select all columns from the 'students' table in the 'students_intro' dataset.
quiz-sql-query-example.hint: Use SELECT and * to get all columns.

help.concept: The SELECT statement retrieves data from a table. Using * selects all columns.
help.hint_1: Start with SELECT, then * for all columns, then FROM and the table name.
